turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Requesting Maintenance or Repairs in a Rental Property

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, I’m calling about a problem in my apartment.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Hi there! No problem at all, thanks for calling. I can help you with maintenance issues. Before we get into the details, can I just confirm the address of the property so I’m looking at the right file?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes. 24 King Street. Unit 603.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect, thank you. I’ve got that open now. Alright, what seems to be the issue today?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: The bathroom. Something is wrong.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, the bathroom — thanks for letting me know. When you say something is wrong, is it related to water, electricity, or something else?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Water. But not always.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, water-related but intermittent — that’s helpful. Can you tell me what actually happens when the problem occurs?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Shower is okay. Then suddenly cold. Then hot again.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, so the water temperature changes suddenly while you’re showering. That can definitely be frustrating. Does this happen every time you shower, or only sometimes?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Not every time. Mostly at night.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Mostly in the evenings — that’s an important detail. That can sometimes be related to water pressure or the system heating up. I’ll make a note of that. When did you first notice this happening?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe two weeks ago. But before it was small.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, so it started about two weeks ago and has gradually become more noticeable. Have you changed anything recently, like adjusting the hot water settings or using a new appliance?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No. Nothing new.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s good to know. Just to check, when the water goes cold, does it stay cold for a long time, or does it return to hot fairly quickly?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Few seconds. But sometimes scary.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I understand — even a few seconds of cold water can be unpleasant, especially unexpectedly. I’ll make sure the technician is aware that the temperature changes suddenly. Now, is this happening in the shower only, or also in the sink?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Sink is okay. Only shower.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect, that helps narrow it down. That suggests it’s likely related to the shower unit rather than the whole apartment. I’ll log it that way so the right person attends.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Just so you know how the process works, I’ll submit this as a non-urgent maintenance request today. A plumber or technician will usually contact you within one to two business days to arrange a time to inspect it.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: They call me?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, they usually call or text first to organise access. Would weekday daytime access be okay, or do you prefer evenings?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Evening is better. After work.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: No problem at all — I’ll note evening access preferred. And just to double-check, is there any leaking water, strange noises, or visible damage around the shower?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: No leak. No noise. Just temperature.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s very clear, thank you. That information really helps us diagnose the issue faster. I’ve now logged the request with those details.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You should receive a confirmation email shortly with a reference number. Please keep that email in case you need to follow up later.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Email is fine.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great. If the problem suddenly gets worse — for example, if the water stops completely or becomes dangerously hot — please contact us straight away, as that could be considered urgent.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. I understand.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Before we finish, can I just quickly check — is there anything else in the bathroom or apartment that’s been bothering you, even if it seems small?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Um… the fan. It’s loud.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thank you for mentioning that. Is the fan loud all the time, or only when it’s been on for a while?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Only when long time.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Alright, I’ll add that as an additional note. The technician can take a look at both issues during the same visit, which is usually more efficient.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. That’s good.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect. So just to recap before I let you go: we’ve logged a maintenance request for sudden temperature changes in the shower, mostly in the evenings, and a noisy bathroom fan after extended use. A technician will contact you within one to two business days to arrange an evening visit.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes. That’s correct.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great. Everything is submitted now. Thanks for letting us know, and we’ll get that sorted for you as soon as possible.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. Thank you.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re very welcome. Have a good rest of your day.
